versions.content: The per player icon (a small silhouette symbol) indicates that a value is multiplied by the number of players who started the scenario. This value does not change if a player is eliminated from the game.
Permanent is a keyword. A card with the permanent keyword cannot leave play. It cannot be defeated, discarded, or returned to a player's hand or deck.

Playing a card involves paying its cost and placing it into the play area (or resolving its effect if it is an event). 'Put into play' bypasses the need to pay costs; the card simply enters the play area from its previous out-of-play state.

A player is eliminated from the game if their identity is defeated (reduced to 0 hit points). When a player is eliminated, their hand, deck, discard pile, and all cards they own in play are removed from the game. The remaining players continue the game.
